title: Chicken and andouille gumbo
subtitle: Louisiana stew built on a dark roux with chicken, smoked sausage, and okra
excerpt: A Louisiana gumbo built on a dark, slow-cooked roux: chicken thighs and smoked sausage in a deeply savoury stock thickened with the holy trinity of onion, celery, and green pepper, and finished with okra. The roux is the foundation and the most demanding part, 30 minutes of patient stirring over medium heat until it reaches the colour of dark chocolate.
sourceNotes: Gumbo is a Louisiana Creole dish with documented origins in the eighteenth century, drawing on West African, French, and Native American cooking traditions. The word gumbo derives from a Bantu word for okra. Multiple cross-referenced Louisiana Creole and Cajun cookery sources; the roux method and holy trinity base are documented in Cajun cookery literature from the late nineteenth century onward.

content.text: The roux is the technical centre of this dish. A Cajun dark roux needs 25 to 35 minutes of continuous stirring over medium heat to go from pale blond to a deep mahogany that smells of toasted nuts and earth. The colour should be one shade before you think it looks burnt. This roux tastes nothing like a béchamel roux. It has been pushed to the edge of its tolerance and the result is complex and slightly bitter. It also loses most of its thickening power as it darkens, so the okra and the stock both contribute to the final body of the gumbo.

Do not walk away from the roux. A burnt roux must be thrown out and started again, there is no fixing it. Once the roux is ready, the heat comes off and the vegetables go in immediately, which cools the pan and stops the roux from darkening further.

items.name: Smoked pork sausage
items.prepNote: andouille or smoked kielbasa, sliced into rounds

content.text: Season the {{chicken-thigh}} chicken thighs with salt and pepper. In a large heavy casserole over medium-high heat, brown the chicken skin-side down for 5 minutes until golden. Flip and brown the other side for 3 minutes. Remove and set aside. Add the {{smoked-pork-sausage}} of sausage rounds and brown for 3 minutes. Remove and set aside.

Pour off any excess fat, leaving about 1 tbsp in the pan. Add the {{vegetable-oil}} of vegetable oil and heat over medium heat. Add the {{plain-flour}} of flour and whisk immediately to combine. Cook, stirring or whisking constantly, for 25 to 35 minutes. The mixture will go through stages: pale blond, peanut butter, dark copper, then a deep mahogany the colour of dark chocolate. The finished roux should smell of toasted nuts. Do not stop stirring. If dark specks appear and the smell turns acrid, the roux is burning, discard it and start again.

Remove the pan from the heat and immediately add the {{onion}} onions, {{celery}} celery, and {{pepper-green}} green pepper, stirring to combine. The steam will be intense. Return to medium heat and cook for 5 minutes, stirring, until the vegetables have softened. Add the {{garlic}} of garlic, {{thyme-dried}} of dried thyme, {{cayenne}} of cayenne, and {{paprika-smoked}} of smoked paprika. Cook for 1 minute. Gradually whisk in the warm {{stock-chicken}} of chicken stock. Add the {{bay-leaves}} of bay leaves. Return the chicken and sausage to the pot.

Bring to the boil, then reduce to a low simmer and cook for 45 minutes. Remove the chicken pieces, strip the meat from the bone, and return the meat to the pot. Add the {{okra}} of okra and cook for a further 15 minutes. Remove the bay leaves. Taste and adjust the seasoning. Serve over cooked rice and scatter over the {{spring-onion}} spring onions.

items.fix: Discard the roux and start again; a burned roux cannot be saved and will make the entire gumbo taste bitter
items.cause: Heat too high, or not stirred continuously
items.symptom: Roux has burned, black specks and acrid smell

items.fix: Simmer with the lid off for 20 to 30 minutes to reduce; or make a small separate pale roux and whisk it into the gumbo
items.cause: Roux not dark enough. It lost its thickening power as it darkened, and the okra is not enough

items.fix: Add the okra at the end. Cook it for only the last 15 minutes. Or dry-roast the okra slices first in the oven at 200°C for 15 minutes before adding.
items.cause: Normal, okra releases mucilage when cooked
items.symptom: Gumbo has a slimy texture from the okra

content.text: Gumbo is the state dish of Louisiana. It brings together West African, French, Spanish, and Native American cooking. The word comes from the Bantu ki ngombo, meaning okra. The dark roux is French in origin. The trio of vegetables (onion, celery, green pepper) is the Louisiana take on French mirepoix. Andouille sausage is the Cajun touch. Every cook makes it a bit differently, and arguing about the right version is a local pastime.
